Catalog Description

Cybersecurity Risk. (3-0). Credit 3. Risks in cybersecurity; avoidance, acceptance,

mitigation, or transference strategies; developing reliable cybersecurity risk

assessments to include analysis, categorization, and evaluation; cybersecurity risk

audit frameworks. Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in CYBR 201, CSCE 201, or

CSCE 221; junior or senior classification. Cross-listed with CYBR 403.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Grade of C or better in CYBR 201/CSCE 201,

CSCE 201/CYBR 201, or CSCE 221; junior or senior classification. Cross Listing:

CYBR 403/CSCE 477.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the students will be able to:

Demonstrate  an understanding of the core concepts and practices that

underpin cybersecurity risk assessment.

Describe  advanced and emerging cybersecurity risk issues, including supply

chain security, privacy risks, and critical infrastructure protection.

Integrate  course concepts to assess, classify, and prioritize risks by

completing hands on risk assessments using realistic case studies.

Communicate  the role of cybersecurity risk within enterprise risk

management and deliver actionable insights with strategic recommendations.

Apply  real-world risk management approaches in real-life scenarios,

demonstrating practical reasoning and cybersecurity risk knowledge.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Each student gets  one free pass  for a late submission (up to 3 days) with no

penalty. The free pass must be claimed before the deadline (student must

email the professor). One day late submission will have 10% points deducted,

two days late submission will have 20% points deducted. No later

submissions will be graded. The free pass can be reclaimed for extra credit at

the end of the semester if never utilized (great deal!)

Students with 504 accommodations will get the necessary accommodations

per their accommodation letter

The course consists of 3 main modules:

Module I:  Risk Assessment Foundations

Module II:  Risk Management

Module III:  Controls and Risk Treatment

Additional Course Information

Course Assessments and activities:

Course Project

The course project will be completed in teams. Students in each team will

be assigned to a use case/company to perform a comprehensive cyber risk

assessment for. The project deliverables will include formal

documentations and in-class presentations. The exact deliverables and

rubric for the project will be posted on Canvas when the project is released.

Peer reviews will be part of the project assessment process, 30% of the final

project grade will be allocated to individual contribution.

Assignments

Most assignments are hands-on lab assignments that align with the course

topics. The labs will be accessible through the JBL platform. Students are

required to log into the platform and complete the labs assigned. In Canvas,

you will have reminders of which labs to complete and their deadlines.

Attendance and Active Classroom Participation

Assignments

Most assignments are hands-on lab assignments that align with the course

topics. The labs will be accessible through the JBL platform. Students are

required to log into the platform and complete the labs assigned. In Canvas,

you will have reminders of which labs to complete and their deadlines.

Attendance and Active Classroom Participation

Coming prepared for class is defined as having studied the material

covered in the previous class, arriving to class on time.

10% of the course grade will be assigned to attendance, active classroom

participation and general classroom behavior and etiquette. Here are the

things that can result in points deducted

Missing more than 2 classes without prior notification and acceptable

absence (See attendance policy section)

Constant cell phone use

Arriving more than 5 minutes late for class

Having earbuds/headphones on (even if not being used)

Browsing Internet, playing games or working on other course work

during class time

Random walks in and out of class

Exams

Both the midterm and final exam will assess the students’ understanding of

the concepts discussed throughout the course as well as the reading

materials.

Exams will have two parts, an in-class multiple choice portion and a take

home potion.

While the in-class activities, discussions and workshops will cover a wide

range of the course topics, it is not possible to cover everything in class,

hence students are expected to complete their textbook readings to get a

deeper understanding of the topics and to prepare for the exams.

AI Statement

Unless indicated in the assignment or project and authorized by the professor, the

use of generative AI to complete the work will be considered a violation of academic

integrity. If in doubt about what it means or counts as a utilization of generative AI,

ask the professor prior to attempting the task to avoid any unexpected penalties, in

other words: Ask for permission instead of forgiveness.

Course Specific Attendance Policy

If for any reason you are unable to attend a class you will need to email the

professor for an excused absence. The professor reserves the rights to assess if the

reason is valid or not and request any supporting documentation.

Missing quizzes or exams does not guarantee a makeup unless the reason

involves  significant and unavoidable circumstances, such as severe illness or

emergencies, and is properly documented .
